description:
A recently-hired incident responder has found a suspicious file that may have been
    tampered with during an intrusion.  Management has asked for an immediate, written
    record of the file’s current SHA-256 checksum so that any future modifications can
    be detected quickly.

    What you must do:

    1. Locate the file  
          /home/user/incident_artifacts/suspicious.bin

    2. Calculate its SHA-256 checksum (use any CLI tool you like, e.g. sha256sum).

    3. Create a brand-new log file  
          /home/user/incident_artifacts/suspicious_checksum.log  
       containing exactly TWO lines, in the **precise** format shown below  
       (no additional spaces, blank lines, or header text):

          File: /home/user/incident_artifacts/suspicious.bin  
          SHA256: <the-64-character-hex-checksum>

       •  “File:” and “SHA256:” are case-sensitive and must be followed by a single
          space.  
       •  The absolute path must appear exactly as shown.  
       •  The second line must contain the 64-character hexadecimal hash (lower-case).

    That’s it — once the log file is present and correctly formatted, the job is done.
truth:
<!--
      Files and directories present when the task container starts
    -->
    <preexisting>
        <directory path="/home/user/incident_artifacts" mode="755"/>
        <file path="/home/user/incident_artifacts/suspicious.bin"
              mode="644"
              size="0"/>   <!-- zero-byte file -->
    </preexisting>

    <!--
      Cryptographic ground-truth
    -->
    <expected_checksum algorithm="sha256">
        e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
        <!-- SHA-256 of an empty file -->
    </expected_checksum>

    <!--
      Artefact that must be created by the agent
    -->
    <required_output path="/home/user/incident_artifacts/suspicious_checksum.log" mode="644">
        File: /home/user/incident_artifacts/suspicious.bin
        SHA256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
    </required_output>
name: task_000000_472789a0
